title: Seoul 2-Day City Break
metaTitle: Seoul 2-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 2-day trip to Seoul, South Korea: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Gyeongbokgung Palace and N Seoul Tower Observatory. Prices from $1348 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 2 days discovering Seoul, South Korea — Four distinct seasons: hot, humid summers (Jun-Aug) with a July monsoon, cold dry winters (Dec-Feb) with occasional snow, and short, pleasant spring and autumn shoulder seasons. March to May for cherry blossoms and mild weather, or September to November for crisp air and autumn foliage. This itinerary blends Gyeongbokgung Palace, N Seoul Tower Observatory, Bukchon Hanok Village with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
1 night accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Gyeongbokgung Palace, N Seoul Tower Observatory, Bukchon Hanok Village
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Seoul
itinerary.description: Land at Incheon International Airport (ICN) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. With limited time on the ground, head straight out to Gyeongbokgung Palace — Main royal palace with changing-of-the-guard ceremony. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Seoul Highlights & Departure
itinerary.description: Start early with a visit to N Seoul Tower Observatory — Namsan Mountain viewing deck. Grab a last taste of local life before your private transfer to ICN for your onward flight.

travelTips: Use Naver Map or KakaoMap, not Google Maps, which has limited functionality in Korea
Get a T-money card immediately on arrival
Tipping is not expected in restaurants or taxis
Convenience stores (GS25, CU) are excellent for cheap, quick meals
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Insulated jacket and warm layers for cold weather
Layered clothing for a four-season climate
Comfortable shoes for extensive subway walking
Compact umbrella for the July monsoon season
Portable WiFi or a local SIM for navigation apps
thingsToAvoid: Overpaying unlicensed taxis outside Incheon airport instead of using official taxi stands
Keeping shoes on in traditional accommodations and some restaurants
Assuming English is widely spoken outside tourist districts
topRestaurants: Gwangjang Market Food Stalls — Korean street food ($)
Pierre Gagnaire à Séoul — French fine dining ($$$$)
luxuryExperiences: Private hanbok photoshoot with palace access
Suite stay at The Shilla Seoul
Private DMZ tour with expert guide
honeymoonExperiences: Spa day at a luxury jjimjilbang
budgetExperiences: Street food crawl at Gwangjang Market
Explore Hongdae street art and busking

festivalCalendar.name: Boryeong Mud Festival (popular day trip from Seoul)

localEtiquette: Bow slightly and use two hands when giving or receiving items, money, or business cards.
Remove shoes before entering homes, temples, and many traditional restaurants.
Pour drinks for others rather than yourself, and turn away slightly when drinking with elders.
Tipping is not customary and can cause confusion in restaurants and taxis.
Avoid writing a person's name in red ink, which is associated with death.
localTransportGuide: Buy a T-money card at any convenience store for seamless use across subway, bus, and taxis. The subway is extensive, cheap, and fully signed in English; the Kakao Taxi app is the standard for ride-hailing.
bestTimeToVisit: March to May for cherry blossoms and mild weather, or September to November for crisp air and autumn foliage.
weather: Four distinct seasons: hot, humid summers (Jun-Aug) with a July monsoon, cold dry winters (Dec-Feb) with occasional snow, and short, pleasant spring and autumn shoulder seasons.
visaInformation: Visa-free for up to 90 days for most Western passport holders (US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia) but a K-ETA (Korea Electronic Travel Authorization) is required in advance for visa-exempt travelers; some nationalities need a full visa. Check K-ETA status well before departure.
averageFlightTime: ~10.7h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Seoul?
faqs.answer: Visa-free for up to 90 days for most Western passport holders (US, UK, EU, Canada, Australia) but a K-ETA (Korea Electronic Travel Authorization) is required in advance for visa-exempt travelers; some nationalities need a full visa. Check K-ETA status well before departure.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Seoul?
faqs.answer: March to May for cherry blossoms and mild weather, or September to November for crisp air and autumn foliage. Four distinct seasons: hot, humid summers (Jun-Aug) with a July monsoon, cold dry winters (Dec-Feb) with occasional snow, and short, pleasant spring and autumn shoulder seasons.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Seoul safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Seoul is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Seoul?
faqs.answer: The local currency is KRW. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Elena Silva
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Leeum Museum of Art and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Emma Johnson
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: Our trip to Seoul was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Gyeongbokgung Palace was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Isabella Williams
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Everland and Ihwa Mural Village. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Grace Wang
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Lotte World Tower Seoul Sky and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Priya Botha
reviews.title: Highly recommend this package
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Changdeokgung Secret Garden in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Elena Tanaka
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Cheonggyecheon Stream and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.
